pattern: Scanning the words with a fixed two-word window collects the word that follows every place the given pair appears in order.
hints: Split the text into words and look at each consecutive triple of words.
Whenever the first two words of a triple match the target pair, the third word is part of the answer.
Iterate i from 0 to len-3 over the word list and collect words[i+2] when words[i] and words[i+1] equal first and second.

input: alice is a good girl she is a good student
we will we will rock you

edge_inputs.description: pair appears only at the very end with no third word
input: the cat sat on the mat the cat

edge_inputs.description: first matches but second never follows
input: a b a c a d

stress.description: long random sentence from a small vocabulary so the pair recurs often
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    vocab = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'good', 'we', 'will']
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 200)))
    words = [vocab[rng.randrange(len(vocab))] for _ in range(n)]
    text = ' '.join(words)[:1000].rstrip()
    first = vocab[rng.randrange(len(vocab))]
    second = vocab[rng.randrange(len(vocab))]
    return [text, first, second]
stress.note: small vocabulary maximizes bigram repeats; text trimmed to constraint length
